tool.name: MAGs-visualization drep-cluster-annot
tool.description: Create a bar chart of dRep clusters with optional annotation
tool.command:
mags-visualization drep-cluster-annot --drep '$drep' --gtdb '$gtdb' --top_n '$top_n' --tax_levels_space '$tax_levels_space' --format '$format'
#if $checkm2 --checkm2 '$checkm2' #end if
#if $quast --quast '$quast' #end if
#if $bakta --bakta '$bakta' #end if
#if $tax_levels --tax_levels #for $tl in $tax_levels '$tl' #end for #end if
@FIG_SIZE_COMMAND@
-o outputs && mv outputs/drep_cluster_*.${format} outputs/final_plot.png

param.label: dRep cluster table
param.help: Use the unmodified dRep cluster table, typically the Cdb.csv output. The table must contain 'genome' and 'secondary_cluster'; each row assigns one genome to one dRep cluster.

param.label: GTDB annotation table
param.help: Use the unmodified GTDB-Tk summary/annotation table containing 'user_genome' and 'classification'. The representative genome taxonomy is shown next to each cluster bar.

param.label: CheckM2 table (optional)
param.help: Optional CheckM2 quality_report.tsv output. The table must contain at least 'Name', 'Completeness', and 'Contamination'.

param.label: QUAST table (optional)
param.help: Optional unmodified QUAST report table with assembly statistics. The table is expected in the ususal QUAST wide format with metrics as rows and assemblies/genomes as columns.

param.label: Bakta table (optional)
param.help: Optional unmodified Bakta annotation summary table. The table is expected in wide format with annotation categories as rows and genomes as columns.

param.label: Taxonomy levels
param.help: Taxonomic ranks from the GTDB classification to display beside each dRep cluster, for example phylum and genus.

param.label: Top N clusters
param.help: Number of largest dRep species-level clusters to display, ranked by the number of member MAGs. Each selected cluster is shown as a horizontal bar.

param.label: Spacing between taxonomy columns (cm)
param.help: Horizontal spacing between the taxonomy annotation columns in cm. Increase this value if taxonomy labels overlap.

tool.help:
**MAGs-visualization: drep-cluster-annot**
This tool visualizes the top N dRep species-level clusters ranked by the number of member MAGs, annotated with GTDB taxonomy of the representative genome.
Each cluster is displayed as a horizontal bar showing the number of MAGs it contains. Taxonomic labels (e.g. phylum, genus) of the cluster representative are shown alongside each bar.
**Input files**
- **dRep cluster table** (``--drep``): use the unmodified dRep cluster table, typically the ``Cdb.csv`` output. The tool uses the ``genome`` and ``secondary_cluster`` columns to count how many MAGs are assigned to each cluster. Additional dRep columns can remain in the file and are ignored when they are not needed.
Example::
genome,secondary_cluster,threshold,cluster_method,comparison_algorithm,primary_cluster SRR24759596_bin_37640.fasta,1_1,0.050000000000000044,average,ANImf,1 SRR24759600_bin_174663.fasta,1_1,0.050000000000000044,average,ANImf,1 SRR24759601_bin_46010.fasta,1_1,0.050000000000000044,average,ANImf,1
- **GTDB-Tk annotation table** (``--gtdb``): use an unmodified GTDB-Tk summary/annotation table containing ``user_genome`` and ``classification``. Genome identifiers are normalized internally before matching them to the dRep, CheckM2, QUAST, and Bakta tables. The example below shows the relevant columns only; the original GTDB-Tk output may contain additional columns and can be used unmodified.
Example with the relevant columns shown::
user_genome classification SRR24759597_bin_73064.fasta d__Bacteria;p__Pseudomonadota;c__Gammaproteobacteria;o__Enterobacterales;f__Enterobacteriaceae;g__Frischella;s__Frischella perrara SRR24759598_bin_119213.fasta d__Bacteria;p__Pseudomonadota;c__Gammaproteobacteria;o__Enterobacterales;f__Enterobacteriaceae;g__Gilliamella;s__Gilliamella sp945271295
- **CheckM2 quality report** (``--checkm2``, optional): use the unmodified ``quality_report.tsv`` output from CheckM2, not the ``protein_files`` or ``diamond_files`` outputs. The tool uses the MAG identifier plus quality columns such as ``Completeness`` and ``Contamination`` for the representative-genome annotations.
Example::
Name Completeness Contamination Completeness_Model_Used Translation_Table_Used Coding_Density Contig_N50 Average_Gene_Length Genome_Size GC_Content Total_Coding_Sequences Total_Contigs Max_Contig_Length Additional_Notes SRR24759597_bin_73064.fasta 99.57 1.16 Neural Network (Specific Model) 11 0.868 33844 315.7918598660484 2115342 0.34 1941 93 151911 None SRR24759598_bin_119213.fasta 85.98 0.91 Neural Network (Specific Model) 11 0.867 47949 307.840271877655 2504613 0.34 2354 61 195230 None
- **QUAST report** (``--quast``, optional): use an unmodified QUAST report table. The expected format is the usual wide QUAST table with assembly metrics as rows and genome/assembly IDs as columns. The example below is shortened to the first few genome columns; the full table can be used unmodified.
Example, shortened to the first genome columns::
Assembly SRR24759597_bin_73064.fasta SRR24759598_bin_119213.fasta SRR24759598_bin_132977.fasta # N's per 100 kbp 0.00 0.00 0.00 # contigs 93 61 720 GC (%) 34.02 34.33 59.77
- **Bakta summary table** (``--bakta``, optional): use an unmodified Bakta annotation summary table. The expected format is a wide table with annotation categories as rows and genomes as columns. The example below is shortened to the first few genome columns; the full table can be used unmodified.
Example, shortened to the first genome columns::
#KEY SRR24759597_bin_73064.fasta_2 SRR24759598_bin_119213.fasta_2 SRR24759598_bin_132977.fasta_2 Annotation Count Count Count CDSs 1874 2297 4820 CRISPR arrays 0 1 0 hypotheticals 51 180 327
**Parameters**
- **Taxonomy levels**: GTDB ranks shown beside each cluster bar, for example phylum and genus. - **Top N clusters**: number of largest dRep clusters to show, ranked by MAG count. - **Spacing between taxonomy columns**: horizontal spacing between taxonomy labels in **cm**; increase this value if labels overlap.
**Output**
- Horizontal bar plot of top N clusters by MAG count, with taxonomic and optional quality annotation.
**Layout options:** All size and spacing parameters (figure width, height, spacers) are specified in **cm**.
Example output plots are available in the MAGs-visualization use cases with example configurations and parameter choices: https://github.com/usegalaxy-eu/MAGs-visualization/tree/main/use-cases
For a direct example (bee microbiome dataset): https://github.com/usegalaxy-eu/MAGs-visualization/tree/main/use-cases/bee-use-case/plots
